visible_hero_h1: Phantom is a technology-led creative agency crafting experiences for global brands.
primary_cta_candidates: Free Spirits Center We created Free Spirits Center as a fully-imagined digital wellness space for Ca7riel & Paco Amoroso’s latest release. Built as a playful, satirical journey through a serene clinic, the experience invites users into a delightfully unhinged world of absurd therapies and strange diagnoses, poking fun at “wellness that only works sometimes”. An intake form asks users to ponder questions like “How do you feel about stillness?”, before offering an immersive prescription that teases track snippets from the album. Following a creative pivot away from architectural 3D spaces, we developed an abstract Therapy Void centred around a responsive orb visualiser and restrained graphic language that sat somewhere between wellness branding, performance art and internet chaos. Built on a flexible development framework and powered by a scalable weighted scoring system, the experience remained highly adaptable throughout production while delivering personalised, surreal outcomes. Sony Music; Powering Purpose Monocle has been synonymous with remarkable print quality since their founding in 2007. For Powering Purpose, their in-depth partnership with Hitachi Energy, Monocle offered their platform to one of the defining issues of our time: the journey towards a safe clean energy transition. We ran with the opportunity to create a scalable design, based on the book, but with its own responsive digital feel. Rather than replicating the book’s content like-for-like, we treated this as a translation exercise: capturing the essence of the book’s feel and content, while adding in unseen stories and additional audio. Monocle; Transforming Business Transforming Business is a groundbreaking playable film experience created to uniquely showcase Google Cloud products to a Financial Times audience. Players step into a year in the life of one of four C-suite leaders at the fictional Sunrise Cereals, navigating five high-pressure business decisions. The experience unfolds in first person, a choice designed to heighten immersion. Mirroring the real pressures faced by today’s executives, a live share price reacts in real time to every decision. By combining raw strategic thinking with the emotional weight of leadership, the experience drives stronger recall and a deeper connection to the realities of modern business. Financial Times; Google Visitor Experience Guide Back in 2023, we supported Google’s pivotal public opening of their Mountain View campus by translating their ethos of innovation and community into an end-to-end brand identity. Now, we’ve created an enhanced digital guide that combines wayfinding with light gamification to take visitors of all ages on an even richer journey, tailored to their interests. The experience needed to be intuitive for first-time guests, engaging for returning visitors, and scalable enough to grow with the campus for years to come. Life-sized artworks facilitated by Burning Man Project and local creators quickly became anchor points for exploration, alongside pop-up shops and thoughtfully designed communal spaces. Using users’ devices' geo-location capabilities in combination with Google’s Routes API, we were able to give directions to points of interest from anywhere on the campus — allowing visitors to start their journey whenever they wanted or even switch routes mid-journey. Google; Google Cloud BigQuery To build awareness for the autonomous data-to-AI platform, BigQuery, we partnered with Google Cloud to create a series of sharp, attention-grabbing films. Crafted with hyper-targeted messaging, we dug into the realities of our audience: busy IT professionals juggling an increasingly tangled web of problems and processes. The first films, released in January 2025, generated standout engagement. But we were eager to push further, aiming for an even sleeker, more impactful second round to spotlight BigQuery’s powerful agentic capabilities. Google; Pixel Singapore Takeover Is it just us or are new phone launches starting to feel a bit vanilla? New colourways, slightly better cameras; nothing special. Not the Google Pixel 10. Packed with Gemini AI, it’s human, helpful, and feels refreshingly, actually, new. To celebrate its launch, we partnered with Pixel’s APAC team to transform their ‘Ask more of your phone’ campaign into an attention-grabbing station takeover that stopped commuters in their tracks. We crafted each execution around tonally provocative headlines that challenged predictable product cycles, showcasing how Pixel’s Gemini-powered features supercharge everyday, relatable moments. Grounded in authenticity and local flavour, we treated each asset as its own micro-story. This approach allowed us to spotlight new Gemini AI features in the wild, weaving them into the mess, magic, and momentum of real life. This isn’t innovation for innovation's sake; it's an upgrade that is actually meaningful. Google; Johnnie Walker Festive Greetings After partnering with Diageo to launch the first-ever AI-powered whisky personalisation experience at Johnnie Walker’s Edinburgh flagship store, we’ve taken our Halo tool all over the world, and collaborated with amazing partners across the whole brand portfolio. So it felt special to be back where it all started at Princess Street with Johnnie Walker, just in time for Christmas. Armed with learnings from our latest experiments, we took on a new challenge: simultaneously creating festive bottle design for both Johnnie Walker Black and Johnnie Walker Blue. It was important that both options felt premium, while still differentiating the offer. Diageo; Doja Cat: Club Vie From the intergalactic fantasies of Planet Her, to the neon haze of the 1980s, our work with Doja Cat is always pushing boundaries. For her fifth studio album, Vie, we devised a WebGL-powered wild night out that enticed fans from the boudoir to the dance floor. Fans' data formed the basis for a virtual guestlist. Doja superfans were able to bypass the fierce bouncers and enter Club Vie before anyone else, giving those good girls and boys early access to exclusive track previews. Using WebGL, we allowed fans to control the track previews by spinning the disco ball, adding a deliciously tactile element. We built the dance floor, dancers, disco ball, and smoke effect separately to replicate the entrancing, hectic atmosphere of a real club. Hidden among the clickable easter eggs is a message from Doja herself, but you’ll have to find that for yourself… au revoir. Sony Music; The Amigos Bottle What excites us most about working with Diageo’s breakthrough innovation team is how relentless they are in their pursuit of the new; a shared commitment that’s always pushing us to new heights. Together, we’ve built a trailblazing approach to personalisation for Casamigos – starting on the white sands of California’s Huntington beach. In the run up to our launch at the US Open of Surfing, we reimagined every aspect of our pioneering customisation engine, Halo, to bring their “house of friends” philosophy to life. To create a style that could stretch across the full spectrum of the Casamigos brand, we focused on elements that offer longevity and flexibility, building flowing gradients and a system of hand-drawn illustrated icons. Casamigos; Snap[Back] To The Year 2000 A playful Snap Lens that brought Smirnoff Ice’s nostalgic 2000s campaign to a whole new audience of Gen Z fans. To mark 25 years of Smirnoff Ice, we transformed Snapchat into a nostalgic Y2K photobooth. Riffing on pop culture icons, early internet energy and getting-ready vibes, users began with a playful time-travel intro before unlocking a randomised filter moment across makeup, backgrounds and frames. Smirnoff; Bridget Jones OOH No queuing for a ticket, unlimited free snacks, and no one talking through the best bits. There’s a reason we love at-home movie nights. For the release of Bridget Jones: Mad About The Boy, we partnered with NBCU to bring the magic to life once more with a pitch perfect campaign that stands out even in a world of endless streaming options. When crafting the copy lines, we took inspiration from the iconic locations they would be displayed, such as the London Bridge motion board. The city has always been the backdrop for Bridget’s escapades, so it felt right to play with location-specific lines. NBC Universal; AI Compass In 2024, businesses were getting lost in a sea of information about AI. Big promises, conflicting information and confusing terminology. The global software tech company SAP wanted to help educate and inform its C-suite audience with a visually engaging online experience presented in partnership with the FT. Our ambition was to elevate a functional educational tool, creating an experience that felt surprising, memorable, and premium for our busy audience. Together with SAP, we defined three key areas where AI is changing business: finance, human resources and supply chain. To deliver a huge volume of information on these three areas, we devised the concept of an intergalactic expanse filled with planets, turning the literal business landscape into an explorable web of knowledge. Visitors can personalise their experience by toggling between foundational and advanced knowledge, ensuring leaders of all levels gained new perspectives. Financial Times
